summary: Review #2 following external greptile-apps[bot] feedback. Changes address two areas: (1) plugins/RenderPastePipeline now skips middleware with unknown/zero-value PipelinePosition instead of generating orphan filter sections, and (2) policy/ValidatePolicyRules signature changed from (PolicySpec) error to (map[string]string, *field.Path) field.ErrorList for Kubernetes-idiomatic structured validation. Both changes are correct, well-tested, and improve the API surface for future CRD webhook integration (S011).

tests_checklist.item: Edge cases are covered (unknown position, zero-value position, nil/empty maps, empty keys, empty values, both empty key+value)
Table-driven structure with Gomega dot-import and NewGomegaWithT(t)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (sorted iteration, map copying, guard clauses)
Functions are focused and small (single responsibility)
No dead code or commented-out code
SPDX license headers and Feature: CC-0004 tracking present

security_checklist.item: No user input paths (pure functions with typed Go inputs)
No external I/O (all functions are pure)
No injection vectors (INI rendering uses string concatenation on trusted internal data)

architecture_checklist.item: ValidatePolicyRules uses field.ErrorList for K8s webhook compatibility
Unknown middleware positions silently skipped (defensive, no panic)
No Kubernetes API calls in any function
Solution is as simple as possible

fail_fast_checklist.item: ValidatePolicyRules returns early for empty/nil rules
RenderPastePipeline skips invalid middleware before pipeline assembly

defensive_checklist.item: Unknown PipelinePosition values handled via default case

suggested_improvements: Minor: The corev1 import in policy.go is used only by MergePolicies (for LocalObjectReference). If MergePolicies is ever refactored to a separate file, the import can be scoped more tightly. No action needed now.
next_steps: Merge PR — all blocking and major checks pass
Future: When S011 CRD webhooks are implemented, wire ValidatePolicyRules into the webhook validation chain using the field.Path pattern established here
